questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
In a parallel plate capacitor set up, the plate area of capacitor is 2 m2 and the plates are separated by 1 m. If the space between the plates are filled with a dielectric material of thickness 0.5 m and are 2 m2 (see figure) the capacitance of the set-up will be ε0 ________.

(Dielectric constant of the material =3.2)

(Round off to the Nearest Integer)
questions.solution:
The given system can be considered as a series combination of two capacitors. The equivalent capacitance can be given as,

1C=1C1+1C2⇒1C=d2Kε0A+d2ε0A

⇒C=ε0Ad2K+d2=2ε0AdK+d

=2×2ε013.2+1=4×3.24.2ε0

=3.04ε0
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A telegraph line of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi>km</mi></math> has a capacity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>01</mn><mo> </mo><mi>μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>km</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> and it carries an alternating current at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn></math> kilo cycle per second. If minimum impedance is required, then the value of the inductance that needs to be introduced in series is _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>mH</mi></math>. (If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>π</mi><mo>=</mo><msqrt><mn>10</mn></msqrt></math>)
questions.solution:
The frequency given is 0.5 kilo cycle per second ⇒f=500 Hz⇒ω=2πf=1000π rad s-1.

Total capacitance of the wire is C=0.01×10-6×100 F.

Impedance is minimum at resonance. Therefore, for minimum impedance

XL=XC

⇒ωL=1ωC⇒L=1ω2C=110002×π2×0.01×10-6×100=10-1 H=100 mH
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The equivalent capacitance between points A and B in below shown figure will be _____ μF.
questions.solution:
Point 3 and 4 are at same potential, hence we can replace the capacitor with a wire.


Capacitors between points A and 3 are in parallel combination as shown above and their combined capacitance value will be 3C.

This 3C capacitance is in series with C.
The equivalent capacitance of the system between points A and B is, 
CAB=3C×C3C+C=3C4
⇒CAB=34×8 μF=6 μF
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A composite parallel plate capacitor is made up of two different dielectric materials with different thickness (t1 and t2) as shown in figure. The two different dielectric material are separated by a conducting foil F. The voltage of the conducting foil is _____ V.
questions.solution:
Capacitance of each capacitor 

C1=A3ε012=6 Aϵ0 and C2=A4ϵ01=4Aε0

Here, A is area of plate.

Equivalent capacitance is 

Ceq=C1C2C1+C2=6Aε0×4Aε06Aε0+4Aε0=2410Aϵ0

Now, net charge stored is qnet=CeqΔV=2410Aε0100=240Aε0

Potential drop across C2 is ΔV2=240Aε04Aε0=60 V

Thus, voltage of the conducting foil is Vfoil =60 V
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In the circuit shown, the energy stored in the capacitor is n μJ. The value of n is _____.
questions.solution:
The capacitor works as an open circuit in the given configuration.

The potential difference between the two plates of the capacitor will be the difference across the resistors having resistances 3 Ω and 4 Ω.

Hence, the potential difference ∆V across the capacitor can be calculated as follows:

ΔV=46×12-312×12 V=5 V

The formula to calculate the energy stored in the capacitor is given by

U=12C(∆V)2   ...1

Substitute the values of the known parameters into equation (1) to calculate the required energy stored.

U=12×6 μF×5 V2=75 μJ
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A series LCR circuit is connected to an ac source of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>220</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi>Hz</mi></math>. The circuit contain a resistance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> and an inductor of inductive reactance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>X</mi><mi>L</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>79</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>6</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math>. The capacitance of the capacitor needed to maximize the average rate at which energy is supplied will be ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>μF</mi></math>.
questions.solution:
The average rate at which energy supplied i.e. power delivered is maximum in case of the resonance, therefore

XC=XL

⇒1ωC=79.6 Ω

⇒   C=1ω×79.6=12πf×79.6=1100π×79.6=40  μF

Hence, C=40 μF.
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In a meter bridge experiment S is a standard resistance. R is a resistance wire. It is found that balancing length is l=25cm. If R is replaced by a wire of hall length and half diameter that of R of same material, then the balancing distance l' (in cm ) will now be ______________.
questions.solution:
XR=7525=3
R=plA=4plπd2
R'=4pl2πd22=2R
πd22
then XR'=100-ll
100-ll=X2R=32
l=40.00 cm
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
For the circuit shown, the value of current at time t=3.2 s will be ______ A.

[Voltage distribution V(t) is shown by Fig. (1) and the circuit is shown in Fig. (2)]
questions.solution:
From graph voltage at t=3.2 sec is 6 volt.

i=6-51

i=1 A
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A square-shaped wire with a resistance of each side <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> is bent to form a complete circle. The resistance between two diametrically opposite points of the circle in a unit of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> will is,  _____.
questions.solution:
We know that in a parallel combination of resistors the net resistance, 1RP=1R1+1R2+1R3+.... and when they are connected in series then net resistance is, R=R1+R2+R3+......+Rn.  A square-shaped wire with a resistance of each side is, 3 Ω, then the circuit is, 
then the net resistance is 3 Ω.
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In the given figure, the value of V0 will be _____ V.
questions.solution:
Using Kirchhoff's junction rule,

V0-21 kΩ+V0-41 kΩ+V0-61 kΩ=0

3V0-12=0

V0=4 V
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The total current supplied to the circuit as shown in figure by the 5 V battery is _____ A.
questions.solution:
As in this case, 5 Ω & 5 Ω are in parallel hence, their combined resistance value will be 2.5 Ω. Now, this 2.5 Ω with existing 2.5 Ω are in series and hence can be combined to write 5 Ω.

In the final diagram, we can write equivalent resistance across the battery as 2.5 Ω.

Hence, current through the battery is, i=5 V2.5 Ω=2 A
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Resistance are connected in a meter bridge circuit as shown in the figure. The balancing length l1 is 40 cm. Now an unknown resistance x is connected in series with P and new balancing length is found to be 80 cm measured from the same end. Then the value of x will be _____ Ω.
questions.solution:
Applying balanced condition for meter bridge

Initially, PQ=40 cm60 cm=23       ...1

Finally, P+xQ=80 cm20 cm=41      ...2

Dividing equation 2 by (1,

P+xP=4×32=6

⇒1+xP=6⇒xP=5

∴  x=5P=5×4=20 Ω
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The current density in a cylindrical wire of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>6</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The current through the outer portion of the wire between radial distances <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>R</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>π</mi><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>.
questions.solution:
Current density is defined as current per unit cross-section area. Therefore,

i=JA=J×πR2-R24

⇒i=4×106×π×16×10-6×34

⇒i=48π A
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A meter bridge setup is shown in the figure. It is used to determine an unknown resistance R using a given resistor of 15 Ω. The galvanometer G shows null defection when tapping key is at 43 cm mark from end A. If the end correction for end A is 2 cm, then the determined value of R will be Ω.
questions.solution:
Using the conditions of a balanced wheatstone bridge and adding the end correction.

15R=l+eL+e-l+e⇒1543+2=R102-45⇒R=5745×15

⇒R=19 Ω
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A loop <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> of straight edges has six corner points <math><mi>A</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mi>B</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mi>C</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mi>D</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mi>E</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math><mi>F</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> . The magnetic field in this region is <math><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>3</mn><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>i</mi></mrow><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>k</mi></mrow><mo>^</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mi>T</mi></math> . The quantity of flux through the loop <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> (in <math><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></math> ) is _____________
questions.solution:
ϕ=B→.A→=3i^+4k^.25i^+25k^

ϕ=2×25+4×25=175weber
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: As per the given figure, if dIdt=-1 A s-1, then the value of VAB at this instant will be ______ V.
questions.solution:
Given here, dIdt=-1 A s-1

Applying Kirchhoff's voltage law to the circuit,

VA-IR-LdIdt-12=VB

⇒VA-2×12-6(-1)-12=VB

⇒VAB=VA-VB=36-6=30 V
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A 1 m long metal rod XY completes the circuit as shown in figure. The plane of the circuit is perpendicular to the magnetic field of flux density 0.15 T. If the resistance of the circuit is 5 Ω, the force needed to move the rod in direction, as indicated, with a constant speed of 4 m s-1 will be _______10–3 N.
questions.solution:
The force on the rod is given by

F=ilB

⇒F=BvlR×lB

⇒F=B2l2vR   ...(i)

The data given is

R=5 Ωl=1 mv=4 m s-1B=0.15 T

Substituting the values in equation (i)

F=0.152×12×45

=18×10-3 N
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: An insulated copper wire of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn></math> turns is wrapped around a wooden cylindrical core of the cross-sectional area <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>24</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi>cm</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math>. The two ends of the wire are connected to a resistor. The total resistance in the circuit is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math>. If an externally applied uniform magnetic field in the core along its axis changes from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> in one direction to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> in the opposite direction, the charge flowing through a point in the circuit during the change of magnetic field will be _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>mC</mi></math>.
questions.solution:
The induced emf ε in the turns of the coil can be written as

ε=-dφdt=iR   ...1

Simplify equation (1) to obtain the required charge through a point in the circuit.

-dφdt=iR⇒-1R∫φ1φ2dφ=∫idt⇒q=φ1-φ2R   ...2

Where, φ1, φ2 are the magnetic flux associated with the coil from two ends.

As, φ=NBA, from equation (2), it can be written that

q=NARB1-B2   ...3

where, N, A are the number of turns and the cross-sectional area of the core respectively.

Substitute the values of the known parameters into equation (3) to calculate the required charge.

q=100×24×10-4 m2×1.5 T--1.5 T12 Ω=0.06 C×1000 mC1 C= 60 mC
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Two identical conducting spheres with negligible volume have 2.1 nC and -0.1 nC charges, respectively. They are brought into contact and then separated by a distance of 0.5 m. The electrostatic force acting between the spheres is ___×10-9 N.
[Given : 4πε0=19×109 SI unit]
questions.solution:
q=2.1-0.12 nC=1 nC

f=9×109×10-180.52=36×10-9
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The electric field in a region is given by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>E</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mn>3</mn><mn>5</mn></mfrac><msub><mi>E</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>4</mn><mn>5</mn></mfrac><msub><mi>E</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The ratio of flux of reported field through the rectangular surface of area <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> (parallel to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>z</mi></math> plane) to that of the surface of area <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> (parallel to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>z</mi></math> plane) is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>:</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>?</mo></math> [Here <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> are unit vectors along <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>z</mi></math> -axes respectively]
questions.solution:
E→=3E05i^+4E05j^ N C-1

A1=0.2 m2 [parallel to y-z plane]

=A→1=0.2 m2i^

A2=0.3 m2 [parallel to x-z plane]

A→2=0.3 m2j^

Now, ϕa=3E05i^+4E05j^·0.2i^=3×0.25E0

& ϕb=3E05i^+4E05j^·0.3j^=4×0.35E0

Now, ϕaϕb=0.61.2=12=ab

⇒a:b=1:2

⇒a=1
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
A 1 m long copper wire carries a current of 1 A. If the cross section of the wire is 2.0 mm2 and the resistivity of copper is 1.7×10-8 Ω m. The force experienced by moving electron in the wire is _____×10-23 N

(Charge of electron=1.6×10-19C )
questions.solution:
Given here, length l=1 m, current i=1 A, area A=2×10-6 m and resistivity ρ=1.7×10-8 Ω m.

Resistance of wire is R=ρlA=1.7×10-8×12×10-5=1.72×10-2 Ω

Now, electric field in the wire is E=ρJ, where, J=iA is current density.

Thus, E=iρA=iR=1×1.72×10-2 V m-1

Electric force in terms of electric field is given by F=qE

Therefore, force experienced by moving electron is  F=1.6×10-19×1.72×10-2

=1.36×10-21=136×10-23 N
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: An electron revolves around an infinite cylindrical wire having uniform linear charge density 2×10-8 C m-1 in circular path under the influence of attractive electrostatic field as shown in the figure. The velocity of electron with which it is revolving is ______________ ×106 m s-1. Given mass of electron =9×10-31 kg
questions.solution:
The electric field is given by 

E=2kλr

The required centripetal force is 

F=mv2r

The force due to the field is equal to the required centripetal force,

⇒2kλre=mv2r

⇒v=2kλem=2×9×109×2×10-8×1.6×10-199×10-31

=1.6×4×1013=4×2×106 m s-1

= 8 × 106 m s-1
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A long solenoid with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1000</mn><mo> </mo><mi>turns</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> has a core material with relative permeability <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>500</mn></math> and volume <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>3</mn></msup><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></mrow><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>.</mo></math> If the core material is replaced by another material having relative permeability of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>750</mn></math> with same volume maintaining same current of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>75</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> in the solenoid, the fractional change in the magnetic moment of the core would be approximately <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mfrac><mi>x</mi><mn>499</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math> Find the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>.</mo></math>
questions.solution:
M→=magnetic moment m→V
⇒ χH→=m→V
⇒ m→=μr-1NiV
∴ Δmm=[750-1-500-1]NiV500-1NiV=250
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The current required to be passed through a solenoid of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> length and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn></math> turns in order to demagnetise a bar magnet of magnetic intensity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>3</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>–</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> is ________ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>.
questions.solution:
The magnetic intensity is

H=Bμ0-M

For M=0, it can be written

H=Bμ0=ni

The data given is 

H=2.4×103 A m-1l=15×10-2 mN=60

Using the relation, n=Nl and H=ni, the value of the current is,

i=Hn=2.4×103×15×10-260=6 A
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A deuteron and a proton moving with equal kinetic energy enter into to a uniform magnetic field at right angle to the field. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>r</mi><mi>d</mi></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>r</mi><mi>p</mi></msub></math> are the radii of their circular paths respectively, then the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>r</mi><mi>d</mi></msub><msub><mi>r</mi><mi>p</mi></msub></mfrac></math> will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>:</mo><mn>1</mn></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is _____ .
questions.solution:
As we know, radius of circular path followed by charged particle under perpendicular magnetic field is given by,

R=mvqB and KE=12mv2

Therefore,

⇒R=2mKEqB  Then, R∝mq

⇒RdRp=mdmp×qpqd=21×11=2:1

Thus, x=2.
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
